name: Manage Pharmaceutical Supply Chain Risk
description: This process identifies and mitigates supply chain risks specific to the pharmaceutical industry, ensuring compliance with regulatory frameworks such as GxP, GMP, GDP, GCP, and ICH Q10. It involves assessing risks associated with batch traceability, audit trails, electronic signatures, validation documentation, deviation management, and CAPA processes.
sector_specific_outputs: risk assessment reports with focus on patient safety
mitigation plans for supply chain disruptions impacting drug availability
CAPA plans for addressing deviations and non-conformities
sector_specific_kpis: percentage of batches released on time
regulatory_requirements: ICH Q10 for pharmaceutical quality systems
21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records and signatures
compliance_frameworks: ISO 13485 for medical device quality management
notes: All automated decisions must be validated and documented per GxP, with a focus on data integrity and audit trails. Electronic signatures and validation documentation are crucial for compliance. The CAPA process must be integrated into the risk management framework to ensure timely and effective corrective actions.
